title: Faulty Enqueue from Queue using Array

statement.markdown: Given a queue Q represented as an array, implement an enqueue operation. The operation should add an element to the end of the queue.
statement.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, representing the number of operations. The next n lines contain the operation type (1 for enqueue, 0 for dequeue), and the corresponding value (if 1, otherwise, ignore the value).
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the final state of the queue after all operations.
statement.notes: The queue should be an array of integers, where each integer represents the value of a queue element. The queue should be 0-indexed.

examples.explanation: The final state of the queue after all operations.

hints.text: Check if the operation type is 1 before adding the value to the queue.

editorial.title: Editorial for Faulty Enqueue from Queue using Array
editorial.intuition: The queue should be implemented using an array, and the enqueue operation should add an element to the end of the array.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves checking if the operation type is 1 before adding the value to the queue.
commonMistakes: Adding the value to the queue without checking the operation type.
Not incrementing the queue size after adding the value.

functionSignature.cpp: bool enqueue(vector<int>& queue, int n, int operation, int value);
functionSignature.java: public boolean enqueue(int[] queue, int n, int operation, int value);
functionSignature.python: def enqueue(self, queue, n, operation, value: int) -> bool:
